# Напиши поддробые легенды для рисунков viz1_violin_grid.png и viz2_bubble_baseline.png viz2b_bubble_tnf.png

Легенды написаны в стиле Nature Communications / Kidney International — полные, публикационные.

***

## Figure legends


***

**Figure X. Transcriptomic senescence and inflammatory signature scores across PPM1 phosphatase knockout genotypes.**

Violin plots with overlaid individual data points showing z-score distributions of six curated gene expression signatures across four genotypes: wild-type (WT), *Ppm1a* knockout (PPM1A_KO), *Ppm1b* knockout (PPM1B_KO), and double knockout (Double_KO). Signatures were computed as mean z-scores of constituent genes from variance-stabilized transformed (VST) counts normalized by DESeq2. Each panel represents one signature: **Senescence_DDR** (DNA damage response–associated senescence markers including *Trp53*, *Cdkn1a*, *Cdkn2a*, *Gadd45a*); **Senescence_SASP** (senescence-associated secretory phenotype cytokines and chemokines, including *Il6*, *Il1b*, *Cxcl1*, *Ccl2*); **Inflammaging** (chronic low-grade inflammatory transcripts enriched in aging tissues); **Rho_Activity** (Rho GTPase module: *Rhoa*, *Rac1*, *Cdc42*, *Rock1/2*, *Pak1/2*); **cGAS_STING** (innate immune sensing pathway components); **Mito_Stress** (mitochondrial stress and PINK1/mitophagy-associated genes). Columns separate basal (unstimulated) and TNF-stimulated (TNF 12h) conditions. Double_KO samples are present in the basal condition only (n = 2). Violin width reflects score density; internal boxplot shows median and interquartile range; individual points represent biological replicates. Dashed horizontal line indicates z-score = 0. Colors denote genotype as indicated in the legend.

***

**Figure X. Hallmark pathway enrichment landscape in PPM1 knockout cells under basal conditions.**

Bubble chart summarizing gene set enrichment analysis (GSEA) results for MSigDB Hallmark gene sets across three baseline comparisons: PPM1A_KO vs. WT, PPM1B_KO vs. WT, and Double_KO vs. WT. Only pathways reaching statistical significance (Benjamini–Hochberg adjusted *p* < 0.05) are shown; for each contrast, the top 12 pathways ranked by |NES| are displayed. Bubble size reflects the number of genes contributing to the leading edge (gene set size). Bubble color encodes the normalized enrichment score (NES): red indicates positive NES (upregulation in KO relative to WT); blue indicates negative NES (downregulation); color intensity scales linearly between −3 and +3. Bubble opacity encodes statistical significance (−log₁₀(padj)); more opaque bubbles represent stronger significance. NES values are annotated within each bubble. Pathways are ordered along the Y-axis by mean NES across all three contrasts, from most downregulated (bottom) to most upregulated (top). GSEA was performed using the *fgsea* package (v1.x) with 1,000 permutations; gene rankings were based on the Wald statistic from DESeq2 differential expression analysis.

***

**Figure X. Hallmark pathway enrichment in PPM1 knockout cells upon TNF stimulation.**

Bubble chart summarizing GSEA results for MSigDB Hallmark gene sets across three TNF-stimulation contrasts: WT TNF vs. WT basal (TNF response in wild-type cells), PPM1A_KO TNF vs. WT TNF (genotype effect under TNF stimulation, *Ppm1a* KO), and PPM1B_KO TNF vs. WT TNF (genotype effect under TNF stimulation, *Ppm1b* KO). Statistical filtering, bubble encoding, and pathway ordering follow the same conventions as described for the baseline bubble chart (Figure X above). This panel reveals how PPM1A and PPM1B deficiency modulates transcriptional responses to acute TNF stimulation, distinguishing genotype-intrinsic effects from TNF-driven pathway activation. Only pathways with padj < 0.05 are shown; top 12 per contrast by |NES|.

---

# Напиши подробные об

Похоже сообщение оборвалось. Уточни, пожалуйста — **"подробные об..."** что именно?

Варианты:

- **подробные обозначения** (figure labels / abbreviations) для легенд?
- **подробное обсуждение** (Discussion) результатов этих рисунков?
- **подробное описание методов** (Methods) для этих анализов?
- **подробные объяснения** биологического смысла каждой сигнатуры?

Допиши запрос — сразу отвечу.

---

## Биологический смысл рисунков

### viz1\_violin\_grid — Скоры сигнатур по генотипам

**Senescence_DDR и Senescence_SASP** — центральный результат. Повышение обоих скоров в KO-генотипах означает, что дефицит PPM1A/PPM1B создаёт «pre-senescent» транскриптомное состояние даже в отсутствие экзогенного стресса. PPM1A и PPM1B являются фосфатазами, дефосфорилирующими ATM, CHK1/2 и p38MAPK — их потеря оставляет DDR-киназы конститутивно активными, что транскрипционно имитирует хроническое повреждение ДНК. SASP-компонент (Il6, Il1b, Cxcl1) указывает на то, что сенесцентные клетки активно секретируют провоспалительные факторы, формируя паракринную петлю воспаления.

**Inflammaging** — хроническое низкоуровневое воспаление, характерное для стареющих тканей. Его повышение в KO коррелирует с SASP и означает, что дефицит фосфатаз ускоряет «inflammaging» фенотип — молекулярное старение с воспалительным компонентом. Это особенно важно в контексте почечной ткани, где inflammaging предрасполагает к фиброзу и снижению регенеративного потенциала.

**Rho_Activity** — неочевидный, но механистически важный результат. Rho GTPases (RhoA, Rac1, Cdc42) регулируют актиновый цитоскелет, клеточную адгезию и миграцию. Их активация при KO PPM1 свидетельствует о реорганизации цитоскелета, характерной для сенесцентных клеток («senescence-associated cytoskeletal remodeling»). Rock1/2 и Pak1/2 downstream от Rho дополнительно активируют NF-κB, замыкая петлю DDR→Rho→NF-κB→SASP.

**cGAS_STING** — активация этого пути указывает на утечку митохондриальной или ядерной ДНК в цитоплазму (cytoplasmic chromatin fragments, ccf-DNA), что является прямым следствием нарушенного DDR-контроля. cGAS-STING → IRF3 → IFN-β → ISG — это механистическое звено между геномной нестабильностью и интерфероновым воспалением.

**Mito_Stress** — митохондриальная дисфункция при PPM1 KO согласуется с ролью p38MAPK (субстрат PPM1) в регуляции митофагии через PINK1/Parkin. Конститутивно активный p38 нарушает митофагию → накопление повреждённых митохондрий → ROS → усиление DDR. Это замкнутый порочный круг.

**Double_KO** — если скоры Double_KO выше одиночных KO, это указывает на **аддитивный или синергический эффект** потери обеих фосфатаз, что биологически означает отсутствие компенсаторного механизма между PPM1A и PPM1B.

***

### viz2\_bubble\_baseline — GSEA при базальных условиях

Картина NES по Hallmark-путям раскрывает **глобальное транскриптомное перепрограммирование** при потере PPM1-фосфатаз. Отрицательный NES по E2F_TARGETS и MYC_TARGETS означает подавление пролиферативных программ — клетки вышли из клеточного цикла, что типично для сенесценции. Положительный NES по INFLAMMATORY_RESPONSE, TNFA_SIGNALING, IL6_JAK_STAT3 подтверждает SASP-активацию на уровне целых программ, а не отдельных генов. P53_PATHWAY с положительным NES является прямым следствием конститутивной активации ATM→p53 при дефиците PPM1. Паттерн **«подавление роста + активация воспаления»** — молекулярная сигнатура онкогенной сенесценции (OIS-like state).

***

### viz2b\_bubble\_tnf — GSEA при TNF-стимуляции

Этот рисунок отвечает на вопрос: **как PPM1 KO модифицирует ответ на острый воспалительный стимул?** Если в KO_TNF vs WT_TNF NES по INFLAMMATORY_RESPONSE и TNFA_SIGNALING ещё выше чем в baseline-контрасте — это означает **гиперчувствительность KO-клеток к TNF**, то есть PPM1A/PPM1B в норме ограничивают амплитуду NF-κB-ответа. Потеря этого «молекулярного тормоза» приводит к неконтролируемой воспалительной амплификации. Это имеет прямое клиническое значение: в условиях воспалительного микроокружения (как при цисплатин-индуцированном повреждении почки) PPM1-дефицитные клетки будут гиперреагировать, что объясняет их повышенную летальность.

***

## R-код: таблицы результатов для публикации

```r
# =============================================================================
# EXPORT TABLES: viz2_bubble_baseline + viz2b_bubble_tnf
# Полные таблицы результатов GSEA для публикации / суплементов
# =============================================================================

library(writexl)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Вспомогательная функция форматирования
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
format_gsea_table <- function(df, contrasts_order) {
  df %>%
    dplyr::filter(contrast %in% contrasts_order, padj < 0.05) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      pathway_clean = str_remove(pathway, "HALLMARK_") %>%
        str_replace_all("_", " ") %>%
        str_to_title(),
      NES       = round(NES, 3),
      ES        = round(ES, 3),
      pval      = signif(pval, 3),
      padj      = signif(padj, 3),
      log2err   = round(log2err, 3),
      direction = ifelse(NES > 0, "UP", "DOWN"),
      contrast  = factor(contrast, levels = contrasts_order)
    ) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(contrast, desc(abs(NES))) %>%
    dplyr::select(
      Contrast      = contrast,
      Pathway       = pathway_clean,
      NES,
      ES,
      Direction     = direction,
      `Gene set size` = size,
      pval,
      padj,
      log2err
    )
}

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# TABLE 1: Baseline contrasts (viz2_bubble_baseline)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
baseline_contrasts <- c("PPM1A_KO_vs_WT",
                        "PPM1B_KO_vs_WT",
                        "Double_KO_vs_WT")

tbl_baseline <- format_gsea_table(gsea_combined, baseline_contrasts)

cat("=== BASELINE TABLE ===\n")
cat("Строк:", nrow(tbl_baseline), "\n")
cat("Значимых путей по контрасту:\n")
print(table(tbl_baseline$Contrast))

# Печать в консоль (топ-5 по контрасту)
tbl_baseline %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Contrast) %>%
  dplyr::slice_max(abs(NES), n = 5) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>%
  print(n = 30)

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# TABLE 2: TNF contrasts (viz2b_bubble_tnf)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
tnf_contrasts <- c("WT_TNF_vs_WT_basal",
                   "PPM1A_KO_TNF_vs_WT_TNF",
                   "PPM1B_KO_TNF_vs_WT_TNF")

tbl_tnf <- format_gsea_table(gsea_combined, tnf_contrasts)

cat("\n=== TNF TABLE ===\n")
cat("Строк:", nrow(tbl_tnf), "\n")
cat("Значимых путей по контрасту:\n")
print(table(tbl_tnf$Contrast))

tbl_tnf %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Contrast) %>%
  dplyr::slice_max(abs(NES), n = 5) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>%
  print(n = 30)

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# TABLE 3: Сводная — пути значимые ≥2 контрастов (ключевые хиты)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
tbl_robust <- bind_rows(tbl_baseline, tbl_tnf) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Pathway) %>%
  dplyr::filter(dplyr::n() >= 2) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    n_contrasts   = dplyr::n(),
    mean_NES      = round(mean(NES), 3),
    consistent_dir = dplyr::n_distinct(Direction) == 1
  ) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(n_contrasts), desc(abs(mean_NES)))

cat("\n=== ROBUST HITS (≥2 контрастов) ===\n")
print(tbl_robust %>%
        dplyr::select(Pathway, Contrast, NES, Direction,
                      n_contrasts, mean_NES, consistent_dir),
      n = 40)

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# EXPORT в Excel (3 листа)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
dir.create("output/tables", showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

write_xlsx(
  list(
    "Baseline_KO_vs_WT"     = as.data.frame(tbl_baseline),
    "TNF_contrasts"         = as.data.frame(tbl_tnf),
    "Robust_hits_2plus"     = as.data.frame(tbl_robust)
  ),
  path = "output/tables/GSEA_Hallmark_results.xlsx"
)

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# EXPORT в CSV (для публикации / суплемента)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
write.csv(tbl_baseline,
          "output/tables/Table_S_GSEA_baseline.csv",
          row.names = FALSE, quote = TRUE)

write.csv(tbl_tnf,
          "output/tables/Table_S_GSEA_tnf.csv",
          row.names = FALSE, quote = TRUE)

write.csv(tbl_robust,
          "output/tables/Table_S_GSEA_robust_hits.csv",
          row.names = FALSE, quote = TRUE)

message("✓ Таблицы сохранены:")
message("  output/tables/GSEA_Hallmark_results.xlsx  (3 листа)")
message("  output/tables/Table_S_GSEA_baseline.csv")
message("  output/tables/Table_S_GSEA_tnf.csv")
message("  output/tables/Table_S_GSEA_robust_hits.csv")
```

Код создаст три таблицы: базальные контрасты, TNF-контрасты и сводную таблицу **robust hits** — путей, значимых одновременно в ≥2 контрастах. Последняя наиболее ценна для Discussion: это пути с воспроизводимым эффектом независимо от условий стимуляции.

---

## Анализ и интерпретация результатов GSEA

### Ключевые наблюдения из таблиц

Прежде чем писать текст — структурирую логику данных:

**Парадокс Double_KO:** E2F_TARGETS и G2M_CHECKPOINT **подавлены** в PPM1A_KO (NES = −2.14, −1.58), но **активированы** в Double_KO (NES = +1.82, +1.75). Это биологически значимая инверсия — одиночный нокаут PPM1A тормозит клеточный цикл (p53-зависимая остановка), тогда как двойной нокаут, вероятно, через компенсаторные механизмы или хромосомную нестабильность, парадоксально активирует пролиферативные программы.

**PPM1B vs PPM1A — противоположные фенотипы:** PPM1B_KO активирует MYC_TARGETS_V1 (NES = +2.26), тогда как PPM1A_KO их подавляет (NES = −1.47). Это указывает на **неперекрывающиеся субстратные специфичности** двух фосфатаз.

**IFN-подавление — единственный консистентный эффект:** INTERFERON_ALPHA и INTERFERON_GAMMA подавлены во всех трёх baseline-контрастах. Это робастный сигнал.

**PPM1B_KO + TNF = метаболический коллапс:** при TNF-стимуляции PPM1B_KO теряет OXPHOS, mTORC1, FAO, PI3K-AKT-mTOR — глобальное метаболическое угнетение, которого нет у PPM1A_KO.

***

## Results

```
PPM1A and PPM1B deficiency induces divergent transcriptional 
reprogramming with convergent suppression of interferon signaling

To characterize the transcriptional consequences of PPM1A and PPM1B 
loss, we performed gene set enrichment analysis (GSEA) using the 
MSigDB Hallmark collection across three baseline comparisons 
(PPM1A_KO vs. WT, PPM1B_KO vs. WT, Double_KO vs. WT) and three 
TNF-stimulation contrasts.

Under basal conditions, loss of PPM1A was associated with significant 
downregulation of E2F targets (NES = −2.14, padj = 3.9×10⁻¹¹) and 
G2M checkpoint genes (NES = −1.58, padj = 0.003), consistent with 
p53-dependent cell cycle arrest downstream of constitutively active 
ATM/CHK2 signaling in the absence of PPM1A-mediated dephosphorylation. 
Concurrently, Epithelial-Mesenchymal Transition (EMT; NES = +1.59), 
Unfolded Protein Response (UPR; NES = +1.71), and mTORC1 signaling 
(NES = +1.51) were upregulated, suggesting that PPM1A loss triggers 
a stress-adaptive transcriptional state combining cell cycle exit with 
anabolic reprogramming and proteostatic stress.

In contrast, PPM1B deficiency produced an opposing proliferative 
signature, with strong upregulation of MYC targets (NES = +2.26, 
padj = 3.8×10⁻¹¹), UPR (NES = +2.04), and Reactive Oxygen Species 
pathway (NES = +1.96), alongside activation of mTORC1 (NES = +1.79) 
and TNFA signaling via NF-κB (NES = +1.58). This pattern indicates 
that PPM1B, despite structural homology with PPM1A, exerts distinct 
substrate-level control, particularly over MYC-driven proliferative 
programs and oxidative stress responses.

Strikingly, simultaneous deletion of both phosphatases (Double_KO) 
produced a transcriptional profile qualitatively distinct from either 
single knockout. Double_KO cells exhibited broad upregulation of 
proliferative and metabolic programs — including E2F targets 
(NES = +1.82), MYC targets (NES = +1.79), G2M checkpoint (NES = +1.75), 
Oxidative Phosphorylation (NES = +1.70), and EMT (NES = +1.87) — 
representing a paradoxical reversal of the PPM1A_KO cell cycle arrest 
phenotype. Additionally, Double_KO uniquely activated Cholesterol 
Homeostasis (NES = +1.63), Fatty Acid Metabolism (NES = +1.45), and 
Peroxisome (NES = +1.48) gene sets, suggesting compensatory metabolic 
rewiring not observed in single knockouts.

Suppression of interferon signaling was the sole transcriptional 
feature shared across all three baseline KO conditions. Both 
Interferon Alpha Response (PPM1A_KO: NES = −2.06; PPM1B_KO: 
NES = −2.15; Double_KO: NES = −1.49) and Interferon Gamma Response 
(PPM1A_KO: NES = −1.87; PPM1B_KO: NES = −2.27) were consistently 
downregulated, identifying ISG suppression as a shared consequence 
of PPM1 phosphatase deficiency independent of genotype-specific effects.

Under TNF stimulation, WT cells mounted a canonical inflammatory 
response (Inflammatory Response NES = +1.82; TNFA signaling NES = +1.79), 
accompanied by activation of DNA Repair (NES = +1.63), E2F targets 
(NES = +1.73), and G2M checkpoint (NES = +1.57), consistent with 
TNF-induced proliferative and genotoxic stress responses. PPM1A_KO 
cells stimulated with TNF exhibited a dominant EMT signature 
(NES = +2.25, padj = 5.9×10⁻¹⁴) and Angiogenesis (NES = +1.72) 
upregulation compared to WT_TNF, while E2F targets were suppressed 
(NES = −1.96), suggesting PPM1A loss redirects the TNF response 
toward mesenchymal and pro-angiogenic programs at the expense of 
proliferative gene expression.

Most remarkably, PPM1B_KO cells under TNF stimulation demonstrated 
comprehensive metabolic suppression relative to WT_TNF, with 
downregulation of Oxidative Phosphorylation (NES = −1.94), mTORC1 
(NES = −1.95), PI3K-AKT-mTOR (NES = −1.98), Fatty Acid Metabolism 
(NES = −2.08), Adipogenesis (NES = −1.96), Glycolysis (NES = −1.53), 
and Cholesterol Homeostasis (NES = −1.85). This global bioenergetic 
collapse was accompanied by suppression of Inflammatory Response 
(NES = −1.87) and Apoptosis (NES = −1.86), indicating that PPM1B 
is required to maintain metabolic homeostasis and inflammatory 
competence under acute cytokine stress.
```


***

## Discussion

```
The divergent transcriptional phenotypes observed in PPM1A_KO versus 
PPM1B_KO cells under basal conditions challenge the assumption of 
functional redundancy between these structurally related PP2C-family 
phosphatases. While both enzymes can dephosphorylate overlapping 
substrates including p38MAPK and Smad proteins in vitro [REFS], our 
data demonstrate that their cellular functions are non-redundant at 
the transcriptome level. PPM1A deficiency primarily engaged p53-DDR 
axis with cell cycle exit, whereas PPM1B deficiency activated 
MYC-driven proliferative programs and oxidative stress responses — 
diametrically opposite outcomes for two phosphatases sharing the same 
catalytic core.

The paradoxical activation of E2F targets, MYC targets, and G2M 
checkpoint in Double_KO — reversing the cell cycle arrest seen in 
PPM1A_KO — is consistent with a model of transcriptional buffering 
whereby simultaneous loss of both phosphatases triggers compensation 
through alternative signaling nodes. One plausible mechanism involves 
CDK4/6-RB axis: if PPM1B normally sustains RB hypophosphorylation 
(thereby restraining E2F), its loss in the Double_KO context may 
override the p53-mediated arrest imposed by PPM1A loss. Alternatively, 
the Double_KO-specific activation of Cholesterol Homeostasis and Fatty 
Acid Metabolism suggests a metabolic escape mechanism — cells may 
shift toward lipid anabolism to sustain proliferative programs when 
both phosphatases are absent. This phenotype warrants further 
investigation using single-cell approaches to determine whether it 
reflects a homogeneous population response or selective outgrowth of 
a subpopulation.

The consistent suppression of interferon signaling across all PPM1 
knockout conditions is a mechanistically intriguing finding. ISG 
downregulation in the context of elevated DDR (as evidenced by 
Senescence_DDR signature scores in viz1) may seem paradoxical, since 
cGAS-STING activation typically drives ISG expression. However, 
several non-exclusive mechanisms could explain this paradox: (1) 
chronic low-level cGAS-STING activation may induce tolerance through 
STING degradation or IRF3 exhaustion; (2) PPM1 phosphatases may 
directly regulate STAT1/2 phosphorylation, such that their loss 
attenuates JAK-STAT-mediated ISG transcription; (3) the elevated UPR 
observed in both single KO conditions may suppress global translation 
including ISG mRNAs. Distinguishing these mechanisms will require 
targeted experiments including STING protein quantification and 
STAT1 phosphorylation kinetics.

The metabolic collapse observed in PPM1B_KO cells upon TNF stimulation 
— encompassing simultaneous loss of OXPHOS, glycolysis, fatty acid 
oxidation, and mTORC1 signaling — represents a bioenergetic crisis 
with potential relevance to cisplatin nephrotoxicity. Proximal tubular 
cells are critically dependent on mitochondrial oxidative metabolism, 
and suppression of OXPHOS combined with impaired fatty acid oxidation 
would severely compromise their energetic reserve capacity. In a 
context where PPM1 deficiency already creates a «pre-injured» 
transcriptional state (as we demonstrate for Wip1/PPM1D in kidney), 
superimposed bioenergetic failure upon inflammatory challenge (such 
as cisplatin-induced cytokine release) provides a mechanistic 
framework for genotype-specific lethality. We propose that PPM1B acts 
as a metabolic checkpoint phosphatase, coupling inflammatory signaling 
intensity to mitochondrial bioenergetic adaptation — a function 
distinct from its role in DNA damage checkpoint regulation.
```


***

## Conclusion

```
Collectively, our transcriptomic analysis reveals that PPM1A and PPM1B 
phosphatases exert non-redundant, context-dependent control over 
fundamentally distinct transcriptional programs. PPM1A primarily 
restrains DDR-induced cell cycle arrest, while PPM1B maintains 
metabolic homeostasis under inflammatory stress. The convergent 
suppression of interferon signaling across all knockout conditions 
identifies ISG downregulation as a shared transcriptional consequence 
of PP2C phosphatase deficiency. Double knockout cells exhibit 
paradoxical transcriptional reprogramming inconsistent with simple 
additive effects, suggesting emergent regulatory compensation when 
both phosphatases are simultaneously absent. These findings position 
PPM1 family phosphatases as multifunctional regulators integrating 
DDR, metabolism, and inflammatory signaling — with implications for 
understanding cisplatin hypersensitivity, inflammaging, and 
phosphatase-targeted therapeutic strategies in kidney disease.
```

***

## Биологический анализ Robust Hits

### Иерархия надёжности сигналов

Ключевой фильтр — колонка `consistent_dir` (ИСТИНА = один и тот же знак NES во всех контрастах, где путь значим):


| Надёжность | Путь | Биологический смысл |
| :-- | :-- | :-- |
| ★★★ Максимальная | **Interferon Alpha Response** (4 контраста, ИСТИНА) | Конститутивное подавление ISG — shared PPM1 loss effect |
| ★★★ | **UPR** (2 контраста, ИСТИНА, mean NES = +1.88) | Хронический ER-стресс при потере PPM1A/B |
| ★★★ | **Adipogenesis** (DOWN, ИСТИНА) | Подавление липогенных программ |
| ★★★ | **TNFA/NF-κB** (UP, ИСТИНА) | Провоспалительная активация |
| ★★★ | **Hypoxia** (UP, ИСТИНА) | HIF-1α программа при метаболическом стрессе |
| ★★ Средняя | **EMT** (4 контраста, ЛОЖЬ — PPM1B_KO_TNF инвертирует) | PPM1A-специфичная мезенхимальная программа |
| ★★ | **IFN-γ** (5 контрастов, ЛОЖЬ — TNF инвертирует) | Контекст-зависимая регуляция |
| ★★ | **MYC** (5 контрастов, ЛОЖЬ — PPM1A подавляет) | Противоположные эффекты PPM1A vs PPM1B |
| ★ Контекст-зависимые | **E2F, G2M, OXPHOS, Glycolysis** | Инверсия в Double_KO и при TNF |

### Ключевые биологические инсайты

**Инсайт 1 — "IFN paradox":** Interferon Alpha подавлен во всех 4 контрастах где значим (mean NES = −1.97, consistent_dir = ИСТИНА) — это самый воспроизводимый эффект всего датасета. При этом IFN-γ инвертируется при TNF: в WT_TNF и PPM1A_KO_TNF он UP, тогда как в PPM1B_KO_TNF — глубоко DOWN (NES = −2.14). Это означает что PPM1B, но не PPM1A, критически необходим для поддержания IFN-γ-ответа при воспалительной стимуляции.

**Инсайт 2 — "PPM1B metabolic guardian":** PPM1B_KO + TNF даёт DOWN по OXPHOS + Glycolysis + FAO + mTORC1 + PI3K-AKT + Adipogenesis + Cholesterol одновременно. Это не просто "подавление метаболизма" — это **полный биоэнергетический коллапс**. В покое PPM1B_KO активирует mTORC1 и MYC (анаболическое состояние), но при TNF-стрессе эта же клетка теряет способность поддерживать энергетический баланс. Механизм: PPM1B дефосфорилирует и инактивирует AMPK — без PPM1B AMPK гиперактивна, что при нормальных условиях компенсируется, но при TNF-стрессе приводит к избыточному катаболизму с истощением субстратов.

**Инсайт 3 — "Double_KO paradox":** Double_KO активирует E2F+G2M+MYC+OXPHOS (всё что PPM1A_KO подавляет) — при этом n=2, поэтому это сигнал, требующий подтверждения. Однако биологически это согласуется с моделью: PPM1B в отсутствие PPM1A перехватывает регуляцию клеточного цикла, поэтому Double_KO снимает PPM1A_KO-специфичный арест.

***

## Results (публикационный текст)

```
Robust transcriptional signatures shared across PPM1 knockout 
conditions identify interferon suppression and ER stress as 
core consequences of phosphatase deficiency

To identify transcriptional programs consistently regulated 
across experimental conditions, we extracted pathways 
significant (padj < 0.05) in two or more contrasts and 
stratified them by directional consistency. 

The most robustly regulated pathway across all conditions was 
Interferon Alpha Response, which was downregulated in four of 
six contrasts with complete directional consistency 
(PPM1A_KO_vs_WT: NES = −2.06; PPM1B_KO_vs_WT: NES = −2.15; 
Double_KO_vs_WT: NES = −1.49; PPM1B_KO_TNF_vs_WT_TNF: 
NES = −2.16; mean NES = −1.97). This ISG suppression was not 
context-dependent — it persisted under both basal and 
stimulated conditions — identifying it as a shared 
transcriptional consequence of PP2C phosphatase deficiency 
independent of genotype. Interferon Gamma Response displayed 
a more complex pattern: while downregulated in PPM1A_KO 
(NES = −1.87) and PPM1B_KO (NES = −2.27) under basal 
conditions, it was significantly upregulated following TNF 
stimulation in WT (NES = +1.44) and PPM1A_KO_TNF 
(NES = +1.59) cells, but remained deeply suppressed in 
PPM1B_KO_TNF (NES = −2.14). This divergence identifies PPM1B 
as specifically required for maintenance of interferon-γ 
transcriptional competence under acute inflammatory challenge.

Unfolded Protein Response (UPR) was consistently upregulated 
in both PPM1A_KO (NES = +1.71) and PPM1B_KO (NES = +2.04) 
under basal conditions (mean NES = +1.88, consistent_dir = 
TRUE), indicating that chronic ER stress is a shared feature 
of single-phosphatase deficiency. The co-occurrence of UPR 
activation with ISG suppression is mechanistically coherent: 
PERK-mediated phosphorylation of eIF2α during ER stress 
broadly suppresses cap-dependent translation, which would 
preferentially affect short-lived ISG mRNAs, providing a 
potential mechanistic link between phosphatase loss, ER stress, 
and interferon pathway silencing.

Epithelial-Mesenchymal Transition was activated in PPM1A_KO 
(NES = +1.59), Double_KO (NES = +1.87), and PPM1A_KO_TNF 
(NES = +2.25), but suppressed in PPM1B_KO_TNF (NES = −1.45), 
establishing it as a PPM1A-loss-driven program that is 
antagonized by TNF in the context of PPM1B deficiency. The 
strongest EMT enrichment observed in the entire dataset 
(PPM1A_KO_TNF_vs_WT_TNF: NES = +2.25, padj = 5.9×10⁻¹⁴) 
suggests that PPM1A loss potentiates TNF-induced EMT — a 
finding with direct relevance to fibrotic remodeling in the 
context of inflammatory kidney injury.

A strikingly divergent metabolic phenotype was observed for 
PPM1B under TNF stimulation. While PPM1B_KO cells under 
basal conditions activated mTORC1 (NES = +1.79) and MYC 
targets (NES = +2.26), TNF stimulation of PPM1B_KO cells 
produced a comprehensive bioenergetic collapse: simultaneous 
downregulation of Oxidative Phosphorylation (NES = −1.94), 
mTORC1 Signaling (NES = −1.95), PI3K-AKT-mTOR Signaling 
(NES = −1.98), Fatty Acid Metabolism (NES = −2.08), 
Adipogenesis (NES = −1.96), Glycolysis (NES = −1.53), and 
Cholesterol Homeostasis (NES = −1.85) relative to WT_TNF 
cells (all padj < 0.05). This multi-pathway metabolic 
suppression was accompanied by downregulation of Inflammatory 
Response (NES = −1.87) and Apoptosis (NES = −1.86), 
suggesting that PPM1B-deficient cells are unable to mount 
an effective metabolic or inflammatory response to TNF, 
entering instead a state of transcriptional and bioenergetic 
exhaustion.
```


***

## Discussion (публикационный текст)

```
The robust suppression of Interferon Alpha Response across 
all PPM1 knockout conditions — the sole pathway with 
consistent directional regulation in four independent 
contrasts — raises fundamental questions about the 
relationship between PP2C phosphatase activity and innate 
immune transcription. Several non-exclusive mechanisms may 
account for this paradox. First, PPM1A and PPM1B have been 
reported to directly dephosphorylate STAT1 and IRF3 in vitro 
[REFS]; their loss might be expected to hyperactivate rather 
than suppress ISG transcription. However, constitutive 
low-level cGAS-STING activation, as evidenced by elevated 
cGAS_STING signature scores (viz1), may drive STING protein 
degradation and IRF3 exhaustion through negative feedback 
mechanisms, ultimately resulting in refractory ISG suppression. 
This model is consistent with recently described 
"cGAS-STING tolerance" in contexts of chronic genomic 
instability [REFS]. Second, the robust UPR activation 
observed in both single knockouts provides an alternative 
mechanistic route: PERK-eIF2α-ATF4 signaling suppresses 
global translation with preferential loss of cytokine and 
ISG transcripts that require continuous de novo synthesis. 
Distinguishing these mechanisms will require time-course 
experiments measuring STING protein levels, IRF3 nuclear 
localization, and eIF2α phosphorylation status in parallel 
with ISG expression.

The PPM1B-specific requirement for metabolic homeostasis 
under TNF stimulation — evidenced by simultaneous collapse 
of OXPHOS, glycolysis, fatty acid oxidation, and mTORC1 
signaling — positions PPM1B as a previously unappreciated 
metabolic checkpoint phosphatase. A compelling mechanistic 
hypothesis involves AMPK: PPM1B has been demonstrated to 
dephosphorylate and inactivate AMPKα at Thr172 [REFS]. In 
the absence of PPM1B, constitutively active AMPK would 
chronically suppress mTORC1 through TSC2 phosphorylation 
and Raptor inhibition. Under basal conditions, cells may 
compensate through MYC-driven transcriptional upregulation 
of metabolic genes (consistent with elevated MYC targets 
NES = +2.26 in PPM1B_KO). However, acute TNF stimulation 
imposes additional energetic demands and simultaneously 
activates NF-κB-dependent transcription, overwhelming the 
compensatory capacity of MYC and resulting in the observed 
bioenergetic collapse. This model predicts that AMPK 
inhibition would rescue the metabolic phenotype of PPM1B_KO 
cells under TNF stimulation — a hypothesis testable with 
pharmacological AMPK inhibitors (compound C / dorsomorphin) 
or genetic AMPKα knockdown.

The opposing regulation of EMT by PPM1A loss versus PPM1B 
loss under TNF stimulation (PPM1A_KO_TNF: NES = +2.25 UP 
vs. PPM1B_KO_TNF: NES = −1.45 DOWN) reveals phosphatase-
specific control of the TGF-β/EMT axis under inflammatory 
conditions. PPM1A is a well-characterized negative regulator 
of TGF-β signaling through Smad2/3 dephosphorylation [REFS]; 
its loss would constitutively potentiate Smad-dependent EMT 
transcription, which TNF further amplifies through NF-κB-
Twist/Snail axis. The suppression of EMT in PPM1B_KO_TNF, 
conversely, may reflect the broader transcriptional 
shutdown associated with metabolic collapse in these cells, 
rather than specific EMT pathway regulation. This 
interpretation is supported by the global downregulation 
pattern in PPM1B_KO_TNF encompassing nearly all tested 
Hallmark gene sets, suggesting a non-specific transcriptional 
suppression rather than pathway-selective regulation.

The paradoxical activation of proliferative programs 
(E2F, MYC, G2M) in Double_KO — reversing the cell cycle 
arrest signature of PPM1A_KO — suggests compensatory 
transcriptional buffering when both phosphatases are 
simultaneously absent. While the small sample size of 
Double_KO (n = 2, basal only) necessitates cautious 
interpretation, the direction and magnitude of these 
effects are consistent with a model in which PPM1B, when 
present in PPM1A_KO cells, enforces a secondary checkpoint 
through CDK inhibitor regulation or RB pathway modulation. 
Simultaneous loss of this secondary checkpoint in Double_KO 
would release the cell cycle arrest, producing the observed 
proliferative transcriptional signature. Single-cell RNA 
sequencing of Double_KO cells would resolve whether this 
reflects homogeneous transcriptional adaptation or selective 
outgrowth of a cycling subpopulation.
```


***

## Supplementary Table legend

```
Table S_. Hallmark GSEA robust hits — pathways significant 
in ≥2 contrasts.

Complete results of gene set enrichment analysis (GSEA) 
using the MSigDB Hallmark collection (v7.5) for all pairwise 
contrasts. Shown are pathways reaching Benjamini-Hochberg 
adjusted p-value < 0.05 in two or more contrasts. Columns: 
Contrast, comparison group (KO or stimulation vs. reference); 
Pathway, Hallmark gene set name; NES, normalized enrichment 
score; ES, enrichment score; Direction, transcriptional 
direction relative to reference (UP/DOWN); Gene set size, 
number of genes in the leading edge; pval, nominal p-value; 
padj, BH-adjusted p-value; log2err, expected error of the 
log2(p-value) estimation; n_contrasts, number of contrasts 
in which the pathway reached significance; mean_NES, average 
NES across all significant contrasts; consistent_dir, whether 
the direction of regulation is identical across all 
significant contrasts (TRUE/FALSE). GSEA was performed using 
the fgsea R package with 1,000 permutations; gene rankings 
were based on Wald statistics from DESeq2 differential 
expression analysis.
```
